channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Food is so cheap it is practically free. — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food is so cheap it is practically free.

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-03-03 00:58 UTC

OP

The image posted is in AUD, but for those in the US, Lets assume an hourly wage of $15.

$15 USD in the US can buy 25lbs of rice. which is 45,000 calories. that is astounding.

I work minimum wage (AUS) and after 2 hours of work I can afford an entire weeks worth of food.

the first hour affords me actually nearly a months worth of carbohydrate energy (rice, pasta and potato's)

The second hour affords me 3kg of meat. which is more than enough protein for a week.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staple_food

very helpful link to understand there is actually not that much food in the entire world to eat for bulk calories/nutrition. there is about 7 different foods to eat. (wheat, rice, corn, potato, meat, dairy and bananas/plantains in certain regions, honorable mention for beans and lentils as well.)

Early replies (first 3)

61926835
People think they're too good for rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables. They deserve to be poor. For the price of two colas you can buy an entire bag of beans which will feed you for weeks. Stop eating corposlop made in a lab that's marked up 10000%, eat real food.

61926840
>>61926822
>eating is so cheap when you eat like a 13th century

61926847
>>61926822
>food is so heckin cheap when you eat like a medieval Chinese peasant!
>updoots to the left!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61926968
>>61926847
It is tho
>>61926822
I heard Starbucks spends more on health insurance than it does on coffee beans. Fast food is expensive now because we ran out of teenagers.

61926977
>>61926870
They have to load the dishwasher afterward.

61927017
>>61926900
>>61926913
The meat, is meat. I don't know what they add to it to make it taste like it does, but what they add to it is not food, so it shouldn't be the deciding factor on if you purchase or not, I am saying to buy food, not logistics.

61927022
>>61926949
This is not true, In Australia, in 2019 pasta was $1.60 per kg, now it is $1.80

same with rice.

meat has increased by about 30% since then as well.

potato's are still $1kg same as 2019 (buy potato's from country grocers, they are very often $1 per kg.)

nowhere near doubled.

61927028
>>61926822
How the hell is there 1000kcals in that order!?

61927035
>>61927028
You're right to be surprised, because the actual amount of food is not much at all, it is just that the potato is fried in oil, and the burger patty as well.

frying food increases caloric density without adding more food.

it's nothing special, it happens if you fry anything.

61927039
>>61926949
>>61927022
and to add onto this, minimum wage has increased by 25% since 2019 as well (with most jobs actually paying closer to the equivalent of a 40% increase), so food is actually cheaper or at the least, the same in relativity to minimum wage.

61927069
>>61927039
>1964 minimum wage $1.25
>$1.25 in 1964 quarters is over an ozt of silver
>1 ozt of physical silver right now is $90 but it's really $100 with premium
>a minimum wage paper boy in 1964 was making $100 an hour our money to silver exchange rate

But the number go up right?

61927071
>>61927069
This is minimum wage to silver, I am talking about the minimum wage to food ratio.

61927076
i doubt american wagies take the time to cook real food. aussies usually do.

61927087
>>61927071
Well in the US a pound of rice in 1964 was about 8 cents, now it's averaging a dollar or more. Which is 12x price raise

Meanwhile the minimum wage in my states gone from 1.25 to less than 8 dollars, only a 7x raise

So the average American is only able to buy half as much rice on minum wage now, as they did 60 years ago

Not Australian so I don't know kangaroo numbers

61927090
>>61927076
Whether they cook the food themselves, or pay someone else to do it for them, they are eating the exact same food.

someone who eats a mcdonalds burger, is eating the same food ingredients as someone who makes spaghetti bolognese

the burger: meat, cheese, wheat (bun/bread)
spaghetti bolognese: meat, cheese, wheat (noodles)

the additives that mcdonalds adds is irrelevant as the additives aren't food. so shouldn't be a deciding factor in if you buy the product or not.

61927097
>>61927087
https://www.walmart.com/ip/Great-Value-Long-Grain-Enriched-Rice-20-lb/10315883?classType=VARIANT

0.57 cents per lb

61927110
>>61927097
*$0.57 per lb
or
57 cents per lb

61927141
>>61927022
i just go off my shopping cost, it's close to double in the last 10 years. I wait for specials on most things now

61927146
>>61926872
It's mostly just negated by experience. I settled on about 15 recipes and I've cut so many corners over the years that they're now 10 minute long each. Most of the shit people tell you to do in a recipe does fucking nothing. I WILL break that pasta in two because I'm not doing a pasta summoning ritual that needs to be followed to a t, I'm just putting it in hot water.

61927151
>>61927141
>I wait for specials on most things now
My weekly food expense is about $35, and the food I buy NEVER goes on special.

If the food you buy goes on special I guarantee it is just the staple foods that have been named in this thread, but with a company logo on it.

61927156
>>61927028
Mostly the bread, without it it's below 800 cal.

61927167
>>61927151
mine used to be about $50 and now it's close to $100. Even simple things like lettuce and tomato have doubled in price. Beef mince has gone from like $11 to $15 in a couple of years. Milk and bread are still pretty cheap though

61927171
>>61926822
You're paying for labor, you spend 3-4 hours preparing the food or you pay some one else to do it. They just happen to be more efficient at it so they can churn out way more meals than you for the same 3-4 hours labor.

61927182
>>61927167
>lettuce and tomato have doubled in price
These are not staple food, they have hardly any calories or nutrients at all, these are called culinary foods, foods that are used purely for taste, texture or color. they are not necessary to buy or eat.
>>61927171
yes exactly, you are paying people to make food for you, that is why mcdonalds and so forth costs so much. and if you were to make a burger and put some potato on the side, it is absolutely not going to take 3-4 hours.

61927193
>>61926835
>fresh vegetables
You mean megafarm product that is 99% water and has no nutrients due to stripped soil?

Eating rice and beans will fuck you up, both digestively and psychologically. I don't mind rice and beans every so often, but subsisting off it like some gookxican farmer is no way to live. Not to mention those beans tear my ass UP. No thanks.

61927195
>>61926870
Take a look at the average beaner and then tell me in all honestly that rice and beans is "healthier". It's slop, plain and simple. It's not mcgoynalds, but it is not sufficient for a white man to survive on.

61927198
>>61927097
In bulk, I stand corrected I stand corrected

61927199
>>61927182
Your body needs fiber and vitamins. Lettuce and tomato are good for you dumbass.

61927202
>>61927195
beaners drink so much coke you would not believe it. I'm pretty sure that's why they're all fat

61927205
>>61927193
Potato's are the only vegetable worth eating anyway.

I wouldn't advise for beans either on a regular basis.
>>61927195
The average mexican in the USA is not eating just plain rice and beans.

61927212
>>61927182
i'm not a bodybuilder or athlete or something though, i just eat normal foods like most people. Lettuce and tomato are very common things to buy. Tomatoes have lots of nutrients too
>Tomatoes are nutrient-dense, low-calorie fruits (approx. 18-22 kcal per medium tomato) composed of 95% water. They are an excellent source of vitamin C (19% DV), potassium, vitamin K, and fiber. Rich in antioxidants, particularly lycopene, they support heart health, cancer prevention, and improved skin, acting as a key anti-inflammatory food.

61927214
>>61927199
Fiber = potato's, banana's and pasta.
Vitamins = primarily meat.

There is hardly any vitamins in lettuce or tomato by themselves, and in comparison to meat, it is no competition. so I don't see your point.

61927225
>>61927212
I'm not a bodybuilder either, if you spend $10 on tomatoes you will have maybe 1.5kg or something, maybe 2kg, that is only 400 calories at most, compare that to spending $10 on rice, with the rice you will have 20,000 calories.

and plants are really only good for carbohydrate and vitamin c, for micronutrients meat is hands down the best.

item.title: PYPL merger edition 1 — 85 replies — 154.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
PYPL merger edition 1

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-03-03 00:26 UTC

OP

Here we go!

Early replies (first 3)

61891698

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61903001
VOLUME RECORD

61903514
>>61897932
Stop giving jews money

61904108
I bought the pico bottom but only threw in $1000

61904711
>>61904697
HOLY FUUUUCK
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

61904727
>>61891742
>Ripple Labs will buy PayPal. Screen Cap this.
I don't think so, only because they claimed they didn't have any big acquisitions planned for the rest of the year

61908061
>>61903001
739M SHARE

61909665
no merger.
we are fucked

61909693
>>61909665
the gap will be filled

61909824
>>61904697

61910089
>>61906351
Thanks for playing, baggies.

61910124
>>61909824

61915327
Any news?

61916113
>>61915327
https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/filing-intech-investment-management-llc-purchases-30734-shares-of-paypal-holdings-inc-pypl-2026-02-28/

61918275
Is this still going to 60$? I went all in at 46 because of this thread.

61919240
>>61918275
I Hope return at price of institutional have buy

61919338
>>61892826
>$300
impressive

61919350
>>61918275
>d
the CEO will get his big cut only at $83 dollar share price MINIMUM.
look at warner bros that shit went from below $10 to now beeing baught at $30
If Paypal gets aquired it will be atleast $100

61919773
>>
https://hedgefollow.com/stocks/PYPL
Hope

61921315
>>61919350
I doubt it will go that high.

61923340
>>61923318

61923342
>>61923340

61925981
>>61904727
At the rate they were going, it wasn't going to be a big acquisition, merely a ripple on the pond

61926795
AMZN

61927102
Can someone explain why Paula even exists?? I remember you needed it for eBay back when everything was new. Somehow it morphed into the default asking to payment

>>61924430

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924491
>>61924430
Based. Unfortunately I can’t unsee the Semiticness of that fat little kike.

61924577
>>61924430

yikes. You've got a lot of maturing to do. Diversity is our strength.

61924624
>>61924577
Shouldn't you be fighting in Iran Shlomo?

61924774
>>61924430
what ho, good morrow and fair tidings fellow gentleman of misaligned pigmentation. what folly is this bavarian pish posh on such a fine non-reflective day of work and trade?

>>61924467
das rite!

61927092
>>61924430
Hyperborian Jack will save the market

item.title: >In the original Matrix script, humans were not meant to be mere batteries, but rather components of a massive, distribu… — 2 replies — 3.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>In the original Matrix script, humans were not meant to be mere batteries, but rather components of a massive, distribu…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-03 00:28 UTC

OP

>In the original Matrix script, humans were not meant to be mere batteries, but rather components of a massive, distributed neural network (wetware) to provide computing power for the AI.

Are there companies experimenting with that? Somewhere where there's not so many ethical rules maybe? Would like to invest in our downfall and reduce the workload on RAM

Early replies (first 3)

61926336
>>61924315
maybe sam altman would do it

61927109
>>61924315
closest thing irl is some European AI company that uses living biological neurons made from stem cells to power their AI.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: WOAH What year is it where am I? /biz/?? when did they add an iz? — 90 replies — 104.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
WOAH What year is it where am I? /biz/?? when did they add an iz?

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-03-03 00:07 UTC

OP

WOAH
What year is it where am I?
/biz/?? when did they add an iz?

Early replies (first 3)

61906377
>>61906370
Whatever..
Finance?
Ummm okay
Ill brought you a gift /b/iz/?

61906378
>>61906370
Thats epic wing guy. Idk why its called that, but he's pretty epic.

61906379
>>61906377
Well would you look at that dubs
were off to a great start

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61914231
best content on the 4chinz for years

61914300
I have been using 4chan since 2008 basically every day

61915467
>>61914222

Wasnt his name Optimized Gif Dude

It was low bandwidth versions of popular memes.

61915797
>>61914300
Same but make it 2004 and few months of break once in a while

61917312
>>61906458
>>61907301
I would really love to go back to post dial up but pre smartphone internet world and just stay there, basically everything past that point has been a downgrade and the few things that have improved aren't worth it at all

61917331
The saddest thing is that as you age and age you realize that the new gens will say the same thing. Their real quality is what they know too.

61917585
>incorrect file names
Salvia officinalis in fields

61917849
>>61906370
>jpg

61918416
great thread. i miss /b/.

61918426
This was not spam.

61918486
>>61909212
Anxiety? What? There’s nothing scary about that photo

61918948
>>61906370
Good thread. It's been a while since OP wasn't a faggot

61918968
>>61918416

It’s a cesspit now. Dont even look. Man I’m 46! Where did the time go.

61920110
>>61918968
dang unc, go outside

61920123
Great thread, man.

61920179
>>61906449
>oopsie
winrar

61920392
>>61906370
efg go up. ascend into higher realms of meme existence

61920744
>>61907301
if the world just ended in 2016 we'd be a lot better off
nothing has been good since then

61921246
>>61906387
Kek'd irl

>>61906426
>>61906431
LOL

61921344
>>61906458
It still is. Ever seen that image of the Chinese kid with a boot on his head, then the next image shows that his arm was in the boot? That's you. You can take the boot off your head and laugh at anytime.

61924708
>>61906449
kys

61924733
>>61924708
lol

61924820
samefag

61925046
hell yeah brother

hh

61925061
I'm glad I got to enjoy this bread

61925959
I beg God every day to take me back 20 years.

61926168
>>61906370

61926678
>>61906370
Thank you OP

61927008
so uhhhhhhhhh, bottom October 2027 instead of 2026?

61927064

item.title: Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l… — 38 replies — 38.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l…

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-02 23:59 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid

>decentralized perp and spot exchange
>trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war
>genius level founders. Harvard, MIT and caltech.
>no vc funding from day 1
>31% geneis airdop tokens to users minting millionaires for early usrs.
>97% all platfrom fees spent and buy HYPE tokens from open market and sent to burned wallet.


Holding HYPE is comfy AF

Early replies (first 3)

61918612
>>61918597
Buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES

61918623
>>61918597
>Shilled by literally all of Twitter fags including scammers like Arthur
>Token not needed
Yeah, no.

61918628
>>61918623
why don't people say "stock not needed". its a crypto company and the coin is basically the stock.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918925
>>61918597
shit volume on commodities
have fun getting adled
dot hl are insufferable fags

61919310
>>61918612
HYPEBTC has a nice bullflag setup

61919323
>>61918623
>token not needed

All 97% of all fees generated by the Hyperliquid exchange is used to buy HYPE tokens and gets sent to burn address.
$1.3 Billion is fees so far. All on chain.

0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe

61919354
>>61919323
Is the hype token used as gas for the chain?

61919376
Fuck hyperliquid, they forced cz the good guy to giganuke the market (like he did in 2022 to destroy ftx/sbf), but these fuckers are still alive.
They need to disappear so cz can allow the market to recover.

61919383
>>61919376
I don't hold any hype but I use the exchange on a daily basis and it works better than even centralized exchanges. It's the best product out there.

61919391
>>61919354
Yes. Hype is used as gas on the HyperEVM chain.

There are two chains in Hyperliquid ecosystem.

HyperCore - trading engine. No gas needed. Pay with trading fees which is used to buy and burn HYPE.

HyperEVM - smart-contract layer. HYPE used as bas.

61920112
I was thinking about grabbing a bag in late Jan and then it pumped again. Serves me right for wanting to buy this under 20 burgerbux

61920124
>>61920112
Same, bro. I just hold btc and trade on hyperliquid. I wanted to get myself a good back of hype, it's the best project out there and the team are not niggers. They want hype to go up. This weekends pump is because everybody is using it to trade crypto and commodities.

One of the few chains that is actually used. No marketing, no bullshit kantian philosophy or any kind of german idealism talk like Sergey or Charles hoskinson

61920169
How do I even buy Hype in the US? I need to use a VPN?

61920469
>>61919376

61920537
can i trade spot btc on there?

61920572
>>61920537
yes. Crypto, stocks, commodities... both spot and perps

61920574
Forex too

61921651
>>61920169
it's on Coinbase now starting Feb 2026. Not sure if it's available in USA.

VPN is very useful. expressvpn

61921686
Funny , this thread popped up because hype's about the moon

61921966
>>61918612
tell that to bloomberg you stupid nigger

61921981
>>61918623
>hurr im not going to buy this multi-billion marketcap token because [insert random person i dont like] is in it!!!
You are thinking like a pleb here and will simply miss out on profits this way.

61922017
>>61918597
I only have 250 hype and refuse to buy up here. It has to retrace back to the low 20's at some point.

Also shilling hype here is a waste. This entire board is so underwater on their bags they're in the mariana trenches.
>Link generals
>Avi generals
>BBBY/GME

I said this to myself earlier this year but Jeff and Vitalik are truly the last 2 people in this entire fucking space that still hasn't soul their souls yet

61922034
>>61921981
Look up GMX.

61924490
Sigh
These poor souls

61924506
edgex is better

61924534
>>61921981
why would he want to buy something that is already multibillions?

61924803
>>61918597
>sent to burned wallet.

why are you lying? the tokens arent burned, they are kept in their treasury

they probably will never have a need to touch those tokens, but they arent burned

61924887
>>61918707
So, the gas is, on a direct level paid in USDC. This is why you one-time sign, and can trade without having to sign each time, unlike GMX. Your trading fees are gas.
These trading fees are used to buy & burn hype. That's why the exchange is a fee collecting engine, and purchasing a share in said engine is by buying hype, which also serves as governance.

Also /biz/ is retarded these days, one-shotted by chainlink and not knowing about anything outside of the cult bubble.

61924972
>>61918597
>source, closed

61926502
>>61922017
it literally broke 20 in the january drop , it should be unhindered to high 40s or 50 into a quick retest of idk 35ish into ath or just straight to 60 over some weeks/months.

61926525
>>61924803
it's 2026. Ask AI about HYPE burn address and stop making shit up.

61927011
>>61926502
Nah this is moonboy posting. The regulatory and closed source fud are real and people are quick to point to the 11 man team making trillions of revenue a year as a bullish moat when any logical investor sees that as a massive target for competitors.

The team at least recognizes this hence the $28 million funding a lobbying team for US compliance. But on a strict regulatory angle Lighter edges Hype out simply due to the fact they're structured from the ground up as a US company on top of their VC connections

61927038
>>61927011
must be great for the lighter shareholders

item.title: Oil price — 50 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil price

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-03-02 23:28 UTC

OP

>Street of Hormuz de-facto closed for at 24h
>20-25% of global oil supply cut from market
>Refineries attacked and closed in Saudi Arabia
>Tankers attacked on/near Street of Hormuz
>Tanker insurance fees through the roof
>Regime wants no negotiations with US
>Army and Guards still loyal to Regime
>Bloomberg predicts prices around 100$/barrel Brent

Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

Early replies (first 3)

61925169
Priced in

61925191
>>61925117
>>Regime wants no negotiations with US
Nice try moshi

61925221
>>61925169
How can an indefinite cut of 20-25% of global oil supply be priced in with like 16$ increase since start of 2026?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925507
>>61925221
The US, and by extension the West, control the overwhelming majority of the world's ready supply. Venezuela is no longer fighting the West or flaunting its rules, and Russia is currently falling on its sword and dying, so the only independent suppliers are the Iran/Iraq area, which is partially mitigated by the Saudis and UAE being West-aligned, and Indonesia.

In short, its priced in because fuck you, that's why. Forgetting that America is the World Protagonist is on you, not us.

61925522
How do you guys see this war lasting more than 2 months?
US doesn't have that many missiles to spare.
US won't start an invasion. That would be wildly unpopular, Trump is already one of the least supported US president in history and he needs for there not to be a Democrat sweep in the midterm elections.
US can't afford a full scale war right now.

61925534
>>61925522
>US won't start an invasion. That would be wildly unpopular
>Trump is already one of the least supported US president in history
Based on that logic there should be no war in Iran. The Republican party is neocon driven. The neocons are in control. Trump's inner circle are all neocons. They don't care about anything except getting their war. They got it. They are happy. Fuck the midterms as far as they are concerned.

61925550
>>61925534
A quick clean victory is what he wanted as that would have likely boosted his ratings

61925567
>>61925550
That's what they say every time. Every fucking time. I heard what was coming out of Pete Hegseth's mouth earlier. This isn't going to be a forever war. This isn't going to be another Iraq. But Trump says he's not afraid to "go long", he says "boots on the ground" are an option. The message he is sending is that it's going to take as long as it's going to take. These people are neocons. They sell the war on the premise that it will be easy to win. If it isn't they don't back down.

61925605
>>61925417
>I think the very real risks are being downplayed
Agreed. "Nothing ever happens" has become a crowded trade.

61925615
>>61925482
>kinda late for that
What do you mean late, it's been happening since day 1 of this war. Iran bombed a ton of NATO bases, you think China isn't interested in that? The only question is whether or not China is fully committed to this. They could be bombing those bases for years, forcing NATO to move more and more weapons to the middle of the desert so that they can shoot a 10k drone with a 10mil missile.
And China did supply Iran with a bunch of stuff between these two wars, let's see if they continue, they're trying to be sneaky. Not sure why though, EU isn't sneaky about giving weapons to Ukraine.
>>61925522
>he thinks US decides when it ends
If US doesn't do a land invasion, Iran can just keep bombing those NATO bases forever. Why stop

61925652
>>61925615
Well you had the supreme leader sitting in his house instead of hiding in a bunker when he had about a week's notice that the US was coming to fuck his shit right up. Now you have a situation where they're scrambling to rebuild their leadership. So how is Iran going to be a good proxy without leadership and organization?

61925667
>>61925117
>oil is only up 5%
scroll out retard oil is going up since december

61925671
>>61925117
priced in

61925673
>>61925300
He's talking about the oil that passes the Strait of Hormuz, not the current price action tard.

61925704
>>61925673
I'm fully aware of what he is talking about.
Read his post again:
>Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

61925726
>>61925704
You're right

61925729
>>61925481
BBC News is now reporting that ships have been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. Cat is out of the bag now.

61925761
>>61925117
Wall street fat cats agreed to help dump while trump bombs iran.
Mostly because they know if Oil gets a green dildo then you can kiss your low CPI scores goodbye. Which then means wall street can kiss any hope of low rates goodbye.

That’s what this is ultimately about, FED rates.

61925906
should i go all into oil lads? seems like it should be pumping, but its not...also crypto is going up when it shoudln't be..clown world

61926020
>>61925906
It's not yet. That's the key. It's a bet on if this is going to keep going.

61926068
No more bullshit on CNBC. Iran says the strait is closed due to aids and any ship that passes through will be set on fire.

61926102
>>61926068
HOLY FUUUCCKIN SHIIT MY LONGS ARE POOOOOOOOOMPING

61926691
>>61925117
Price increases would cover insurance premiums going up. If the premiums go up but the price doesn't go up transporting oil becomes unprofitable. This could exacerbate any shortages in the long term.

61926787
>>61925117
>>61925169
>>61925191

Literally nothing is priced in. VIX is 21 and oil barely closed at 72. Market thinks this is a nothingburger.

If you disagree buy 10k worth of VIX or USO calls April expiry and take the 7x. That’s all there is to it. Shits not in the price.

61926796
>>61926691
https://infra.economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/ports-shipping/war-risk-premiums-surge-as-hormuz-crisis-forces-rerouted-sailings-threatening-indias-trade/128933982?dt=2026-03-02

61926861
>>61925117
Based. Total westoid humiliation.

61926893
Let's make this perfectly clear.

https://k.sina.cn/article_1887344341_707e96d504001qa2g.html
Translation:
>According to vessel tracking platform TankerTrackers.com, more than 50 tankers remain in Iranian waters, 18 of which are loaded with crude oil and 37 are empty.

>Premiums are soaring.

But as said in the link here: >>61926796

>On Sunday, GIC Re issued notice of cancellation for its marine hull war risk cover, stating that the cover will cease from March 3. The insurer has drawn up a high-risk area that includes Pakistan waters, Persian or Arabian Gulf and adjacent waters/ports including the Gulf of Oman west of Longitude 58 degree east, Iran and all other countries under sanction by the UN, the UK, the US or the EU, Sea of Azov and Black Sea waters, waters of Russia, Ukraine/ Belarus, India Ocean, Gulf of Aden, and Southern Red Sea.

>“In case any vessel passes through this area or calls any port located in this area or dry docked in any of these areas, it will be a breach of warranty,” GIC Re wrote in a circular issued on Sunday, adding that the breach of warranty cover will not be available in respect of all the seven zones mentioned in the high-risk area.

So if I get this right it didn't even matter that much that Iran actually attacked ships. The threat they made to do so going forward made it impossible to get insurance to pass through. So the strait is effectively closed not just by way of Iran's actions but because of the insurers. The market's optimism today was delusional. This news will spread.

61926901
>>61925534
It’s just a few airstrikes dude, it’s not that deep.

61926908
>>61926901
It won't take long for you to realize the economic damage done.

61926914
>>61926908
To them yeah, not over here. I’m just going to keep profiting from my oil tanker stocks in the meantime.

61926920
>>61926914
lol

lmao even

61926942
>>61925117
token not needed

item.title: Today is the day — 21 replies — 32.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is the day

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-03-03 00:20 UTC

OP

Lunar eclipse is today anons. Last solar eclipse Feb 17th was the local bottom. Full moon/lunar eclipse historically marks the crash day according to Puetz cycle.

BTC is going to crash. A lot of blood. Mercury retrograde just started, we're in the crash window until March 9th. Elliott Wave confirms 3rd of 3rd wave down on SPX which drags everything with it.

And now US/Israel vs Iran is actually popping off. Late Saturday night attack and counter-attack.

Every piece is falling into place at the same time — the astro, the technicals, the geopolitics. This isn't a coincidence.
Alts are getting absolutely destroyed. This isn't random volatility.

DYOR. Not financial advice.

Early replies (first 3)

61923231
>>61923226
Bring me 1500 eth or stfu already pls! Thank you for your attention to this matter!

61923232
>>61923226
Nice Schizo rant

61923238
>>61923231
>>61923232
You can pump this in a matter of hours. Tonight will be a day to remember. Everything has brought us to today. I just want to help all those perma bulls out there and their longs. The ones that listen to shitty youtubers and crypto influencers like Raol Paul, the granny from Ark Investors, etc.

I like /biz/ and just wanna help.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923274
>>61923238
We will see i approtiate your opinion but i dont believe in it.

61923329
Okay bobo

Just shorted 100x on chinkbase

61923339
>>61923329
I'm not a bear. I hold and stack 2 crypto assets. I'm shorting the rest. I'm just telling you because even if /biz/ has become a very slow board, I had a lot of fun with all of you in the past.

61923345
>>61923274
>approtiate

I also don’t believe your opinion, Rakesh

61923357
>>61923226
Thanks brother, but we know
This will be the bottom of the cycle, it began in early 2025
Next blood moon will be seen in 2028, every bull cycle came after a 12 to 18 months long blood moon period

61923389
>>61923345
Its european hours amerimutt.

61924238
>>61923226
Well who could have thought

61924257
>>61923226
>Mercury retrograde
That's some dumb woman shit right there

61924557
Oh well I guess op went silent and is looking for the next schizo date

61924669
>>61923226
>Lunar eclipse is today anons.
Tomorrow

61925218
It's all coming together. 33333

61926891
Op?

61926971
>>61923226
well this thread aged gracefully like all schizo threads do

>>61923238
>crypto influencers like Raol Paul
he seems to be right about the macro impuls turning here now doesnt he, while everyone else is calling for the spx to be bottomless

61926981
>>61923238
>b-but i come to you out of concern!

61927049
>>61923226

61927075
Are you ready for the purim human sacrifice for moshiach, /biz/?

61927077
>>61923226
Astrology is astrology for men.

61927085
>>61923226
Everytime I see OP's picrelated I can't help but think of my neighbor who looks exactly the same and makes the same face whenever she sees me. It's a combination of mostly concern, a bit of disgust and contempt, a touch of latent sexual attraction, and a sprinkle of empathy

item.title: Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock — 39 replies — 31.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-02 23:45 UTC

OP

>Have an easy office job
>Mostly just scroll on my phone
>Literally just do barely anything and make salary
>Realize I could make money in my phone during this time
>Start making AI art, get up to $100 a month from subs
Anyone aware of something that could make more than this and is primarily done on your phone. I do not mind doing it on my PC at home too, as I already do this with AI stuff For now, AI is easy enough and extra money is extra money but more cash flows will always be better
Note, I want actual income, not “Just sell options on your phone while at your job bro”

Early replies (first 3)

61924922
So basically asian tits make me straight haha

61924932
I'm considering getting a 3d printer farm plus I know how to work with the files and make custom things but idk seems effort

61924936
>>61924922
I’m happy for you anon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925575
>>61925564
Nah it requires at least stability

61925594
>>61925207
>Just have 700k
I calculated my breakoff point where I can live entirely off safety nets indefinitely is 865k. The good thing is at least I'm getting closer

61925611
>>61925594
High seven figure hell is real. I want to quit working but Canada is stupid expensive to live in. Probably gonna fuck off to the Caribbeans once I hit $1 million USD

61925619
>>61924922
You're 35. It's time to grow up and have a wife AND kids.

61925623
>>61925611
Oops meant high six figure.

61925624
>>61925611
Well the numbers are based on how much you can extract monthly while still living like a human and not like a nigger so yeah

61925650
I was considering candle making but it's saturated by women

61925663
Hey man, I know it's difficult, but listen, the honest truth is that most phone based side hustles that feel easy are capped low and crowded, so your hundred a month from AI art is actually normal, not underperformance, and the uncomfortable reality is that meaningful extra income usually comes from either building an audience, selling something people repeatedly buy, or doing work others are too lazy or too skilled to do, which means if you want to beat that number from your phone you should focus on things like running niche subscription pages with consistent content drops, flipping underpriced items locally through marketplace apps, managing short form content pages that you later monetize with affiliates, or doing simple but in demand freelance tasks through mobile friendly platforms, because the people who quietly make real side money are not chasing magic passive tricks, they are stacking small repeatable systems and letting them compound while everyone else keeps searching for effortless cash.

61925701
>>61925560
I’m a doctor and found a woman similiar to that but white. I already left her tho because i want to fuck differebt kinds of hotties. So whatevet doctor makes + social status.

61925705
>>61924882
i fucking NEED a gf this hot
how do i do it ? money? looksmaxing?
please someone tell me how

61925774
>>61925705
All you’re going to notice is that boobs are just blobs of fat that smell like fat and look way better than they feel. Also the woman will naturally be rather unimpressed by anything because of how hot she sees herself.

61925778
>>61925705
She's not hot, she just has big tits

61925779
>>61925564
Don't ever say dumb shit like that ever again. Lack of money destroys even the strongest love.

61925806
>>61924882
Making money off of a phone alone just means you are a content creator. Which is a viable side hustle if you have charisma and/or skills and knowledge. Everyone wants a job where they make money with their phone so try something else.

And it's nice having a big titty wife.

61925811
>>61924882
I must rub my cock on a traditionally conservative nubile large breasted Nippon whore

61925831
>>61925774
i dont care all women are whores anyway so i might as well have a hot big titted gf
>>61925778
you are either a homosexual or a jealous roastie

61925844
>>61925705
>>61925774
You don't NEED one anon. Remember you will find no peace with a "hot" woman. Every guy even your friends will be constantly trying to fuck her or messaging her. This will keep you in a state of perpetual paranoia and misery. Hot women are mostly unhinged and mentally unstable. They expect you to do everything in the relationship while offering very little in return. Probably boring unenthusiastic sex too. She will most likely abuse you in the relationship because she sees you as wasting her time when she could be out fucking other chads and gaining maximum attention for her herself. Hot women are out for themselves they know that they have millions of thirsty guys who will fuck them, and they will make you aware of that every day.

61925851
>>61925663
great post

61925903
>>61925806
I'm thinking of side hustle streaming and photography while travelling amerilard on trailer but doing so would require some investment

61925909
>>61925844
nigger i did the average girl route for 10+ years already both roads lead to the same destination at least with the hot girl you get to enjoy it while it lasts

61925926
>>61924922
Asians don't count as straight.

61925995
>>61925844
>>61925909
hot women are also extremely boring and shallow since they don't need to develop social skills

61925999
>>61925995
Wow they're just like me then.

61926357
>>61925508
patreon? how do people pay for ai anime girls

61926427
>>61925508

61926854
>>61925663
Thanks for the rundown
I’m fairly content and have a friend who is making more so if I can scale to 150 or 200 monthly that’s really not bad all things considered

61926904
>>61926427

61926915
I didn't read shit bitch. I just saw tits.

61926931
>>61926904

61926281
>>61926208
BOY I SURE LOVE SI

61926339
>>61926208
for real though HEAR ME, LAND LUBBERS!
THIS BE YE FINAL CALL TO BOARD!
Watch the charts, and plot your course carefully! The seas be stormy, but hold fast, and ye'll make port with a booty greater than any other in this time! It may be many moons but ye'll come up smiling yet!
Watch for the crests of the waves and the troughs, and cast ye line into those dips! However, take heed, this will be your last entry.
The last cup is forming.
Be sure to fill it to the brim (with rum) and slurp deeply!
Lest ye regret.

61926355
jane street = jail jeets

61926390
How the fuck is this price action depressing you faggots

61926425

i expect a little dip, but that only means better plunder!

61926808

61926820

61926827

61926982

nay, it were gold and silver from a box! i speak ye true, plundered some gold and silver recently, found a discarded jewelery box in some trash, had some silver chains and a gold bracelet in it! not massive, but some real loot! tripple digits in value.
